title: Node Contracts Schema
description: Single source of truth for workflow node parameters and outputs

fieldStatus.description: Sync status between frontend and backend

frontendName.description: Name used in frontend if different

backendName.description: Name used in backend if different

outputs.description: Output fields produced by the node

nestedTypes.description: Nested type definitions used by this node

nodes.name: Manual Trigger
nodes.description: Triggered manually by user clicking a button

outputs.name: triggered_at
outputs.notes: ISO timestamp when the workflow was triggered. Emitted snake_case by ManualTriggerResolver, matching node_type_documentation seed (V11).

outputs.name: triggered_by
outputs.notes: Display name of the user who triggered the workflow (resolved via AuthClient.getDisplayName). Empty string when no profile is available. Never the raw tenantId.

outputs.name: {customField.name}
outputs.notes: Dynamic fields from trigger.params(), flattened at root

outputs.name: trigger_id
outputs.notes: Added by TriggerNode for all trigger types

outputs.name: item_id
outputs.notes: Added by TriggerNode for all trigger types

outputs.name: item_index
outputs.notes: Added by TriggerNode for all trigger types

outputs.name: data
outputs.notes: Single-item array with triggered_at + triggered_by + custom fields

nodes.name: Chat Trigger
nodes.description: Triggered when user sends a message matching a pattern

parameters.name: pattern
parameters.notes: Called 'value' internally in some places

outputs.name: extracted_message
outputs.notes: Message with prefix/suffix trimmed based on trimPrefix/trimSuffix config

outputs.name: matched
outputs.notes: Whether the message matched the pattern

outputs.name: match_type
outputs.notes: The match type used (any, starts_with, ends_with, contains, equals, regex)

outputs.name: attachments
outputs.notes: File attachments sent with the message. Each item is a canonical FileRef: { _type:'file', path, name, mimeType, size }

outputs.name: triggered_at
outputs.notes: ISO timestamp when trigger fired (snake_case, unified across triggers)

outputs.name: triggered_by
outputs.notes: Display name of the user whose chat message fired the trigger (empty when unknown). Never tenantId.

outputs.name: data
outputs.notes: Single-item array with message, extracted_message, triggered_at, triggered_by, matched

nodes.name: Webhook Trigger
nodes.description: Triggered via HTTP request to a webhook URL. Body and query params are flattened at root level.

parameters.name: basicUsername
parameters.notes: UI stores in basicAuth.username, serializes to flat

parameters.name: basicPassword
parameters.notes: UI stores in basicAuth.password, serializes to flat

parameters.name: authHeaderName
parameters.notes: UI stores in headerAuth.headerName, serializes to flat

parameters.name: authHeaderValue
parameters.notes: UI stores in headerAuth.headerValue, serializes to flat

parameters.name: jwtSecretKey
parameters.notes: UI stores in jwtAuth.secretKey, serializes to flat

outputs.name: {body_field}
outputs.notes: All HTTP body fields flattened at root level. For GET requests, query params are used instead.

outputs.name: {query_param}
outputs.notes: Query string params merged into root (for non-GET, merged with body; for GET, query only)

outputs.name: _webhookMethod
outputs.notes: HTTP method used (GET, POST, etc.). Added by WebhookController.

outputs.name: _webhookTimestamp
outputs.notes: ISO timestamp when webhook was received. Added by WebhookController.

outputs.name: triggered_at
outputs.notes: ISO timestamp when the webhook fired (snake_case, unified across triggers)

outputs.name: triggered_by
outputs.notes: Display name of the workflow owner. Empty when the webhook is unauthenticated.

nodes.name: Schedule Trigger
nodes.description: Triggered on a schedule defined by cron expression

parameters.name: maxExecutions
parameters.notes: Optional limit on executions

outputs.name: triggered_at
outputs.notes: ISO timestamp of trigger. Raw: triggeredAt (camelCase), SchemaMapper normalizes to triggered_at.

outputs.name: triggered_by
outputs.notes: Display name of the workflow owner. Schedule fires autonomously but still carries the owner identity for variable_mapping.

outputs.name: execution_count
outputs.notes: Current execution number (1-based). Raw: executionCount (camelCase), SchemaMapper normalizes.

outputs.name: next_execution
outputs.notes: Next scheduled execution time after this fire. Raw: nextExecution (camelCase), SchemaMapper normalizes.

outputs.name: cron
outputs.notes: Cron expression from schedule config. From ScheduleExecutorService.buildPayload.

outputs.name: timezone
outputs.notes: Timezone from schedule config. From ScheduleExecutorService.buildPayload.

outputs.name: scheduleId
outputs.notes: UUID of the ScheduledExecutionEntity. From ScheduleExecutorService.buildPayload.

nodes.name: Form Trigger
nodes.description: Triggered when user submits a custom form

outputs.name: submission_id
outputs.notes: Form submission identifier (UUID), generated at submit time by FormDispatchService

outputs.name: submitted_at
outputs.notes: When the form was submitted (ISO timestamp). Set at dispatch time by FormDispatchService.

outputs.name: form_data
outputs.notes: All submitted form fields grouped as object. Populated at dispatch time - never empty when a form is submitted.

outputs.name: triggered_at
outputs.notes: Unified-trigger alias of submitted_at. Same ISO timestamp - use either.

outputs.name: triggered_by
outputs.notes: Display name of the form submitter (empty when anonymous). Never the raw tenantId.

outputs.name: {field_name}
outputs.notes: Dynamic per field definition, also at top level for easy SpEL access

nodes.name: Datasource Trigger
nodes.description: Event-driven trigger: fires one run per row created/updated/deleted in a datasource. Replaces the legacy on-demand loader (use schedule + find for bulk scans).

parameters.name: dataSourceId
parameters.notes: Datasource to subscribe to. Stored in trigger.id.

parameters.name: event_types
parameters.notes: Which row events fire this trigger. Multi-select.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.notes: Optional single-condition filter {column, operator, value}. Operators: eq, neq, gt, gte, lt, lte, contains, starts_with, ends_with, is_null, is_not_null.

outputs.name: row
outputs.notes: The row that triggered the event (current state for create/update, last-known state for delete).

outputs.name: previous_row
outputs.notes: Pre-change row. Populated for row_updated; null for row_created.

outputs.name: row_id
outputs.notes: ID of the row in data_source_items.

outputs.name: datasource_id
outputs.notes: ID of the datasource emitting the event.

outputs.name: triggered_at
outputs.notes: ISO timestamp when the event was emitted (after DB commit).

outputs.name: triggered_by
outputs.notes: Display name of the workflow owner. Empty when the row-event source is a system process.

outputs.name: {column}
outputs.notes: Row columns are also flattened at top level for easy SpEL access ({{trigger.column_name}}).

outputs.name: item_index
outputs.notes: Added by TriggerNode (always 0 - one event = one run)

nodes.name: Workflow Trigger
nodes.description: Triggered when parent workflow completes

parameters.name: workflowId
parameters.notes: Stored in trigger.id

parameters.name: workflowName
parameters.notes: Stored in trigger.input for display

outputs.name: triggered_at
outputs.notes: ISO timestamp when the parent workflow fired this one (snake_case, unified across triggers)

outputs.name: triggered_by
outputs.notes: Display name of the workflow owner. Empty when the parent ran in a system context.

outputs.name: result
outputs.notes: Merged step outputs from the parent workflow run.

outputs.name: parentStatistics
outputs.notes: Execution statistics from the parent workflow run (completedSteps, failedSteps, totalSteps). Present only when the parent recorded statistics.

outputs.name: {parent_key}
outputs.notes: Each output key from the parent's last completed node is also flattened at root level (e.g. {{trigger:child.output.my_parent_key}}). Names vary per parent configuration.

nodes.name: AI Agent
nodes.description: AI-powered agent that can use tools

parameters.name: systemPrompt
parameters.notes: Optional system prompt override

outputs.name: tool_calls
outputs.notes: Count of tool calls made by the agent

outputs.name: tool_calls_detail
outputs.notes: Detailed tool call info (name, input, output)

nodes.name: Classify
nodes.description: AI-powered classification with multiple categories

parameters.name: input
parameters.notes: UI uses classifyInput internally, serializes to input

outputs.name: selected_category
outputs.notes: The category label that was selected by the AI

outputs.name: selected_category_index
outputs.notes: Index of the selected category in the categories array

nodes.name: Guardrail
nodes.description: Content validation and safety checks

parameters.name: rules
parameters.notes: UI uses guardrailRules internally, serializes to rules

parameters.name: input
parameters.notes: UI uses guardrailInput internally, serializes to input

nodes.name: Decision
nodes.description: Conditional branching based on expressions

outputs.name: selected_branch
outputs.notes: Branch type that matched: if, elsif, else

outputs.name: selected_branch_index
outputs.notes: Index of the selected branch (-1 if none)

nodes.name: Loop
nodes.description: While loop with condition

outputs.name: iteration
outputs.notes: Current iteration number (0 on first entry)

outputs.name: enter_body
outputs.notes: Whether the loop body should be entered

outputs.name: reason
outputs.notes: Exit reason, present only once terminated: condition_false or max_iterations_reached

nodes.name: Split
nodes.description: Iterate over a list of items in parallel

parameters.name: list
parameters.notes: SpEL expression for items to iterate. Alias: listExpression (deprecated)

parameters.name: splitStrategy
parameters.notes: FE uses strategy in UI, serializes to splitStrategy

outputs.name: current_item
outputs.description: Current item in parallel branch (runtime context, body nodes only)

outputs.name: current_index
outputs.description: 0-based index of current item (runtime context, body nodes only)

outputs.name: items
outputs.notes: The collection being split (persisted by SplitNodeExecutor for inspection)

outputs.name: terminated
outputs.notes: Always true: split completes immediately after spawning

nodes.name: Merge
nodes.description: Merge multiple branches (AND wait - waits for ALL predecessors)

outputs.name: {strategy_output}
outputs.notes: Dynamic output from merge strategy (QUEUE_1_TO_1, COMBINE_ALL, FIRST_AVAILABLE)

nodes.name: Fork
nodes.description: Parallel fork - ALL branches execute simultaneously (no conditions)

parameters.name: forkOutputs
parameters.notes: Defines parallel branches via :branch_N ports

outputs.name: branches
outputs.notes: List of branch info objects with index, id, label, target_count

nodes.name: Switch
nodes.description: Switch/case branching based on expression value

parameters.name: switchExpression
parameters.notes: Expression to evaluate for case matching

outputs.name: selected_case
outputs.notes: The case type that matched (case_0, case_1, default)

outputs.name: matched_value
outputs.notes: The value of the case that matched

nodes.name: Note
nodes.description: Documentation annotation on the workflow canvas

parameters.name: color
parameters.notes: Background color

nodes.name: Interface
nodes.description: UI interface reference for user interaction. Registers INTERFACE_SIGNAL and may block or auto-advance depending on __continue in actionMapping.

parameters.name: interfaceId
parameters.notes: ID of the interface to render

parameters.name: actionMapping
parameters.notes: Map of button action names to workflow actions (__continue, step keys, etc.)

outputs.name: interface_id
outputs.notes: ID of the interface being rendered

outputs.name: is_entry_interface
outputs.notes: Whether this is the first interface in the workflow

nodes.name: Transform
nodes.description: Transform data using expressions

outputs.name: {mapping.label}
outputs.notes: Dynamic per mapping definition - each mapping label becomes an output key

nodes.name: MCP Tool
nodes.description: Execute an MCP tool/API

parameters.name: paramExpressions
parameters.notes: Map of param name to SpEL expression, serializes to step.input

outputs.name: {dynamicSchema}
outputs.notes: Dynamic per tool response schema. Output comes from ToolsGateway execution.

nodes.name: Wait
nodes.description: Wait for a specified duration. Short waits (<=3s) use inline sleep, long waits register a WAIT_TIMER signal and yield.

parameters.name: duration
parameters.notes: Duration in milliseconds

outputs.name: expires_at
outputs.notes: ISO timestamp when wait expires (signal/yield mode)

nodes.name: Exit
nodes.description: Ends execution along this branch - terminal node. Other parallel branches (fork, split) continue normally.

outputs.name: reason
outputs.notes: Exit reason text from node configuration

nodes.name: Stop on Error
nodes.description: Immediately stops the ENTIRE workflow (all branches) with an error. Unlike Exit (branch-only), this terminates everything and marks the run as FAILED.

parameters.name: errorMessage
parameters.notes: Error message (supports SpEL templates)

parameters.name: errorCode
parameters.notes: Optional error code for programmatic handling

outputs.name: stopped_at
outputs.notes: ISO timestamp when stop was triggered

nodes.name: Response
nodes.description: Sends a message response to chat interface

parameters.name: message
parameters.notes: SpEL expression for the response message

outputs.name: message
outputs.notes: The resolved message that was sent

outputs.name: sent_at
outputs.notes: ISO timestamp when message was sent

nodes.name: Option
nodes.description: Multiple choice branching - evaluates expressions in order, first true wins

outputs.name: selected_choice
outputs.notes: ID of the choice that matched

outputs.name: selected_branches
outputs.notes: Array containing the selected branch label (or empty if none matched)

outputs.name: skipped_branches
outputs.notes: Labels of branches that were not selected

outputs.name: evaluations
outputs.notes: Detailed evaluation results (choice_id, choice_label, expression, resolved_expression, result, error?)

nodes.name: Aggregate
nodes.description: Aggregates data from parallel executions (ForEach) using field expressions

outputs.name: {field.label}
outputs.notes: Dynamic per field definition - each field label becomes an array of collected values

nodes.name: Summarize
nodes.description: Groups items by field(s) and applies aggregation functions (sum, avg, count, min, max, countDistinct, concatenate) per group.

parameters.name: input
parameters.notes: SpEL expression resolving to a list of objects to aggregate.

parameters.name: aggregations
parameters.notes: Each entry: { field, operation, alias }. 'field' is blank for count. 'alias' defaults to operation_field.

parameters.name: groupBy
parameters.notes: Optional field names to group by. Empty = single group over all items.

outputs.name: groups
outputs.notes: Array of group objects. Each group has group_key, group_count, groupBy field values, and each aggregation alias.

outputs.name: {alias}
outputs.notes: When no groupBy is configured, each aggregation alias is also stored at the top level for convenient SpEL access (e.g. {{core:summarize.output.total_salary}}). Alias names vary per configuration.

nodes.name: Limit
nodes.description: Passes through only the first or last N items from a collection, with optional offset

parameters.name: input
parameters.notes: SpEL expression referencing the list to limit, e.g. {{core:step.output.items}}

parameters.name: count
parameters.notes: Maximum number of items to return (default: 10)

parameters.name: from
parameters.notes: Take from the beginning or end of the list (default: first)

parameters.name: offset
parameters.notes: Number of items to skip before applying the limit (default: 0)

outputs.name: count
outputs.notes: Number of items in the limited result

outputs.name: config
outputs.notes: Limit configuration: { input, input_count, count, from, offset }

nodes.name: Download File
nodes.description: Downloads a file from a URL and stores it in object storage

parameters.name: url
parameters.notes: URL to download from (can be SpEL expression)

parameters.name: filename
parameters.notes: Optional filename override

outputs.name: file
outputs.notes: Canonical FileRef {_type:'file', path, name, mimeType, size}. Reference via {{core:label.output.file}} to render in interfaces; marketplace + share previews recognise this shape (showcase HMAC rewriter).

outputs.name: source_url
outputs.notes: Original URL that was downloaded from

nodes.name: Compression
nodes.description: Compresses or decompresses data. Compress produces a file stored in object storage; decompress returns the decompressed string.

parameters.name: operation
parameters.notes: Operation to perform

parameters.name: format
parameters.notes: Compression format

parameters.name: value
parameters.notes: SpEL expression referencing the data to compress/decompress

parameters.name: filename
parameters.notes: Optional output filename (compress only)

outputs.name: result
outputs.notes: The decompressed result (decompress operation only - compress emits `file` instead).

outputs.name: file
outputs.notes: Canonical FileRef {_type:'file', path, name, mimeType, size} (compress only). Reference via {{core:label.output.file}}; marketplace + share previews recognise this shape.

nodes.name: Convert to File
nodes.description: Converts data (list of maps) to a file format (CSV, Excel/XLSX, JSON, TXT) and stores it in object storage

parameters.name: format
parameters.notes: Output file format

parameters.name: value
parameters.notes: SpEL expression referencing the list of maps to convert

parameters.name: filename
parameters.notes: Output filename without extension (default: export)

parameters.name: delimiter
parameters.notes: CSV delimiter (csv only)

parameters.name: includeHeaders
parameters.notes: Include column headers in CSV/XLSX (yes or no)

outputs.name: file
outputs.notes: Canonical FileRef {_type:'file', path, name, mimeType, size}. Reference via {{core:label.output.file}}; marketplace + share previews recognise this shape.

outputs.name: row_count
outputs.notes: Number of rows written to the file

nodes.name: SFTP
nodes.description: File operations on remote servers via SFTP: upload, download, list, delete, rename, mkdir

parameters.name: host
parameters.notes: SFTP server hostname or IP

parameters.name: port
parameters.notes: SFTP server port (default: 22)

parameters.name: password
parameters.notes: Password (authMethod=password)

parameters.name: privateKey
parameters.notes: PEM private key content (authMethod=privateKey)

parameters.name: remotePath
parameters.notes: Remote file/directory path

parameters.name: localContent
parameters.notes: SpEL expression for content to upload (upload only)

parameters.name: newPath
parameters.notes: New remote path for rename operation

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.notes: Connection timeout in milliseconds

parameters.name: credentialId
parameters.notes: Optional stored credential ID

outputs.name: success
outputs.notes: True if the SFTP operation succeeded

outputs.name: files
outputs.notes: Array of file entries for list operation: {name, size, is_dir, modified}

outputs.name: file_count
outputs.notes: Number of file entries returned (list operation)

outputs.name: file
outputs.notes: Canonical FileRef {_type:'file', path, name, mimeType, size} (download operation). Reference via {{core:label.output.file}}; marketplace + share previews recognise this shape.

outputs.name: new_path
outputs.notes: New file path after rename operation

outputs.name: uploaded_size
outputs.notes: Number of bytes written to the remote server (upload operation only)

nodes.name: HTTP Request
nodes.description: Makes HTTP calls to external APIs. Supports GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE, HEAD, OPTIONS with various auth and body types.

parameters.name: url
parameters.notes: URL expression (supports SpEL templates)

parameters.name: authConfig
parameters.notes: Auth configuration: username/password (basic), bearerToken, apiKeyName/apiKeyValue/apiKeyLocation, headerName/headerValue

parameters.name: queryParams
parameters.notes: Array of {key, value} pairs for query string

parameters.name: headers
parameters.notes: Array of {key, value} pairs for custom headers

parameters.name: body
parameters.notes: Body expression (supports SpEL templates)

parameters.name: contentType
parameters.notes: Custom Content-Type header override

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.notes: Request timeout in milliseconds (max 300000)

outputs.name: success
outputs.notes: Whether the HTTP request succeeded (2xx) or failed (4xx/5xx). Workflow continues either way.

outputs.name: data
outputs.notes: Response body (parsed JSON or raw string). Empty object if no body.

outputs.name: error
outputs.notes: Error message (only on HTTP error responses)

outputs.name: input_data
outputs.notes: Snapshot of resolved request params (method, url, body, bodyType, authType) for inspector

nodes.name: Data Input
nodes.description: Provides multiple labeled text and/or file inputs to the workflow. Each item's label becomes an output key.

parameters.name: items
parameters.notes: Array of DataInputItem with label, type (text/file), text, file

outputs.name: {item.label}
outputs.notes: Dynamic per item - text items resolve SpEL, file items pass FileRef object

outputs.name: input_data
outputs.notes: Raw expressions before resolution, for inspector visibility

nodes.name: User Approval
nodes.description: Branching node with ports: approved, rejected, timeout. Registers USER_APPROVAL signal and yields until resolved.

parameters.name: approverRoles
parameters.notes: List of roles allowed to approve

parameters.name: requiredApprovals
parameters.notes: Number of approvals required (multi-level)

parameters.name: timeoutMs
parameters.notes: Timeout in milliseconds (default 24h). Triggers timeout port.

parameters.name: contextTemplate
parameters.notes: Template (literal + {{...}} expressions) resolved at pause time and shown to the approver as approvalContext. Required (warning, non-blocking): the run still works if empty.

parameters.name: delegation
parameters.notes: Optional external-channel delegation: {channel, credentialId, chatId, messageTemplate, allowedUserIds}. channel: 'telegram' only in v1. credentialId: OPTIONAL numeric id; omit = the user's own Telegram credential is resolved implicitly (same as an mcp:telegram step without an explicit credential), a numeric id pins a specific bot. chatId: destination chat id, template-capable ({{...}}). messageTemplate: optional message body, template-capable; blank = falls back to the resolved contextTemplate. allowedUserIds: optional allowlist of channel user ids; empty = anyone in the chat can decide.

outputs.name: selected_port
outputs.notes: approved, rejected, or timeout - set after signal resolution

outputs.name: approval_context
outputs.notes: Resolved contextTemplate shown to the approver, carried into the resolved output (present only when a context template resolved to non-blank text)

outputs.name: delegated_channel
outputs.notes: External channel the approval was delegated to (e.g. telegram). Present only when delegation is configured.

nodes.name: Task
nodes.description: CRUD operations on agent-board tasks from a workflow: create_task, get_task, update_task, delete_task, list_tasks. create_task with agentId auto-triggers the assignee asynchronously (the DAG does NOT wait).

parameters.name: taskId
parameters.notes: Template - UUID of existing task (required for get/update/delete)

parameters.name: title
parameters.notes: Template - task title (required for create, optional for update)

parameters.name: instructions
parameters.notes: Template - task instructions (create/update)

parameters.name: priority
parameters.notes: Priority for create/update, or filter for list

parameters.name: agentId
parameters.notes: Template - assignee agent UUID. If set on create, the agent auto-fires asynchronously

parameters.name: reviewerAgentId
parameters.notes: Template - reviewer agent UUID (create only)

parameters.name: status
parameters.notes: New status for update, or filter for list

parameters.name: search
parameters.notes: Template - title search term (list only)

parameters.name: limit
parameters.notes: Max results for list (1-200)

parameters.name: taskContext
parameters.notes: Arbitrary key/value context attached to the task (create only). String values support templates. Stored as a JSON string in the builder form, as an object in the plan

outputs.name: task
outputs.notes: The task object (create/get/update). status starts pending when agentId auto-triggers; result stays null until the agent finishes

outputs.name: tasks
outputs.notes: Array of task objects (list_tasks only, workspace-scoped)

outputs.name: count
outputs.notes: Number of tasks in the current page (list_tasks only)

outputs.name: resolved_params
outputs.notes: Snapshot of resolved request params for the inspector

nodes.name: Insert Row
nodes.description: Insert new rows in a database table

parameters.name: columns
parameters.notes: Map of column name to value/expression

outputs.name: inserted_values
outputs.notes: Map of column names to inserted values

nodes.name: Get Rows
nodes.description: Get rows from a database table with filters

parameters.name: where
parameters.notes: Filter condition: {column, operator, value}

outputs.name: has_more
outputs.notes: Whether more rows exist beyond limit

nodes.name: Update Row
nodes.description: Update existing rows in a database table

parameters.name: where
parameters.notes: Filter condition: {column, operator, value}

parameters.name: set
parameters.notes: Map of column name to new value

nodes.name: Delete Row
nodes.description: Delete rows from a database table

parameters.name: where
parameters.notes: Filter condition: {column, operator, value}

nodes.name: Create Column
nodes.description: Create new columns in a database table

parameters.name: columns
parameters.notes: Array of {name, type, defaultValue?}

outputs.name: createdColumns
outputs.notes: List of column names created (camelCase, from CrudToolExecutor)

nodes.name: Find Row
nodes.description: Queries a data source and returns matching rows as a collection (does not spawn parallel per-row contexts; use a Split node on the items for that).

parameters.name: table_id
parameters.notes: Datasource ID (alias: dataSourceId)

parameters.name: where
parameters.notes: Filter condition: {column, operator, value}

parameters.name: maxItems
parameters.notes: Maximum number of rows to process. Caps the result set.

outputs.name: has_more
outputs.notes: Whether more rows exist beyond the limit

outputs.name: find_id
outputs.notes: Node ID of the find node
